top_categories.workout_context: 99ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Surrey Rumble, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Surrey Rumble. Train it until it feels boring.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Surrey Rumble's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
At mile 60 of 80, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
7,890ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Surrey Rumble's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 80 miles.
7,890ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Low-cadence force work for 7,890ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 80 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 7,890ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Tempo is Surrey Rumble's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 80 miles on mixed terrain. Train it until it's automatic.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Surrey Rumble’s 80 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on TT Threshold, Over Under, and G Spot to prepare you for 80 miles of mixed terrain in Cobham, Surrey, England.
